metadata.description: Village generation configuration — controls placement, sizing, NPCs, and structures
notes: Villages are placed during geographic world generation
Each village occupies a square area of chunks (size x size)
Walls surround the village with evenly-distributed entrances
NPCs are spawned from templates defined in this file
Village tiers control size, entrances, NPC count, and building density

placement._target_count_effects: HIGHER = more villages across the world. Lower = fewer, more spread out.
placement._min_distance_effects: Minimum chunks between village centers. HIGHER = more spread out.
placement._valid_danger_effects: Danger levels where villages can spawn (1=Tranquil, 2=Peaceful, 3=Moderate).
placement._excluded_effects: Chunk types that cannot contain villages.

tiers._comment: Village tiers define size, structure, and population. Future: tier selection based on location/danger.

small._entrances_effects: Number of wall entrances. Distributed evenly across sides.
small._entrance_width_effects: Width of each entrance in tiles.
small._wall_inset_effects: Tiles inward from chunk edge for wall placement.
small._spawn_weight_effects: Relative probability of this tier being chosen.

entrance_distribution._comment: How entrances are distributed across walls. For N entrances on a 4-sided village:
rules: 4 entrances: one on each wall (N, S, E, W)
5 entrances: one on each wall + one extra on longest wall, spaced evenly
6+ entrances: distributed round-robin across walls, spaced evenly

npc_templates.name: Farmer
dialogue_lines: The soil is good this season.
Have you seen any slimes nearby?

npc_templates.name: Guard
dialogue_lines: Halt! Oh, just a traveler. Carry on.
Keep your eyes open out there.
The roads aren't safe at night.

npc_templates.name: Merchant
dialogue_lines: Looking to trade? ...Not yet, I'm afraid.

npc_templates.name: Scholar
dialogue_lines: Ah, a fellow seeker of knowledge!
I've been studying the local wildlife.
Did you know slimes can split when threatened?
